info.name: Fintech Platform — Backend (dev)
info.description: Manual-test collection for the invoice-discounting backend, against the **dev profile** (`./mvnw spring-boot:run -Dspring-boot.run.profiles=dev`). One-click OTP login per role; the Golden Path folder drives a whole deal `listed → distributed/closed` (incl. TDS withholding + Form 16A) with auto-chained ids/versions and an HMAC-signed inflow webhook. See docs/MANUAL_TESTING.md. Uses the dev-seeded supplier/buyer/investor (run `Dev helpers → seed-info` first). Full endpoint reference: docs/API_CATALOGUE.md.

item.name: 0 · Bootstrap first super_admin (API key)
item.description: Break-glass: mint the first **super_admin** on a fresh DB, then log in — all with just the API key (`{{bootstrap_api_key}}`, from `platform.bootstrap.api-key`), in any profile. Run 1→4 top-to-bottom: bootstrap → password → peek OTP (`/bootstrap/last-otp`, same key, works for any email) → verify → bearer stashed in `bearer_ops`-style `boot_bearer`. The OTP peek reads the in-process stub; once a real SMS provider is wired it self-retires (404) and the code goes to the real phone.

item.name: 1 · Login (one click per role)
item.description: Each request runs password → fetch OTP (dev) → verify, and saves the bearer. If a bearer comes back empty, just send the request once more.

item.name: 2 · Golden Path (run top-to-bottom)
item.description: A whole deal lifecycle. Prereq: run `Dev helpers → seed-info`, then `Login: Ops`, `Login: Treasury`, `Login: Treasury2`. Each request chains the listing id + aggregate-version automatically.

exec: pm.test('has a form_16a statement', () => pm.expect(j.some(s => s.kind === 'form_16a')).to.be.true);

item.name: 5 · Supplier onboarding (M7 · BC8)
item.description: WS-1 supplier lifecycle `created → active`, all admin-on-behalf. Prereq: `Login: Ops`, `Login: Compliance`, `Login: Credit`. Each step chains onb_supplier_id + X-Aggregate-Version automatically. KYC approval is done by Compliance (maker≠checker vs the Ops submitter); credit-review by Credit (SoD).

exec: const pan = L()+L()+L()+L()+L() + String(1000 + Math.floor(Math.random()*9000)) + L();

item.name: NEG · Credit review by Ops → 403 role_not_held
6 · Buyer management (M8 · BC9)
item.description: WS-2 buyer lifecycle `nominated → active`, all admin-on-behalf, plus its OTP-only acknowledgment user. Prereq: `Login: Ops`, `Login: Credit`. Nominate is a Credit action; credit-assessment is Credit (SoD); the rest are Ops. Each step chains onb_buyer_id + X-Aggregate-Version automatically.

exec: const pan = L()+L()+L()+L()+L() + String(1000 + Math.floor(Math.random()*9000)) + L();

item.name: NEG · Credit assessment by Ops → 403 role_not_held
7 · Documents & Invoice Upload (M18/M19)
item.description:
**Everything document-related, in the order an intern should learn it.**

- **A** — the generic 3-step upload (initiate → upload → finalize → read).
- **B** — generic guard rails (idempotency, validation, unknown ids, sealed bytes).
- **C** — the invoice flow: upload a PDF and attach it to a listing.
- **D** — invoice attach negatives (not-stored / unknown / wrong role / duplicate).
- **E** — replace / supersede an attached invoice.
- **F** — KYC documents (advanced; needs a kyc_file_id).

**One-time setup:** run `1 · Login → Login: Ops` and (for C–E) `0 · Dev helpers → seed-info`. See `postman/README.md` for the full walkthrough.

item.name: A · Generic /documents upload — happy path
item.description:
The 3-step BC16 upload every UI/operator uses: **initiate → upload → finalize**, then read.

**Before running:** open `1 · Login → Login: Ops` once (sets `bearer_ops`). Then run A1→A5 top-to-bottom.

The PDF body is plain text starting with `%PDF-1.4` — the server keys off the `application/pdf` *content-type*, not the bytes, so you never need a real file.

item.name: B · Generic /documents — negatives & idempotency
item.description: Each request here is **independent** — run any one on its own (after `Login: Ops`). These prove the guard rails: idempotency, body validation, missing headers, unknown ids, and the 'bytes are sealed until stored' rule.

item.name: B2 Initiate — replay (same X-Command-Id) → same id

exec: pm.test('400 — cannot finalize with no uploaded bytes', () => pm.response.to.have.status(400));

request.description: Spring rejects the wrong content-type at routing (consumes=application/pdf) before the service sees it.

item.name: B7 Upload to an unknown document → 404
C · Invoice upload & attach (BC1) — happy path
item.description:
Upload an invoice PDF, then **attach** its `document_id` to a listing's invoice (M19).

**Before running:** (1) `1 · Login → Login: Ops`, (2) `0 · Dev helpers → seed-info` (sets `supplier_id` + `buyer_id`). Then run C1→C8 top-to-bottom.

C1 creates a fresh **draft** listing so the artifact set is still open (DOC.4). C8 shows the download gate: a draft listing is not downloadable — you get a clean 400 until the deal is live.

item.name: C5 Attach to invoice (Ops, sets idem_cmd) → 2xx
C7 List invoice documents → one active

request.description: Downloads open only once the listing reaches the live-set. Run the '2 · Golden Path' to take a listing live, then a GET on its attached doc returns 200 bytes.

exec: pm.test('400 — not eligible until listing is live', () => pm.response.to.have.status(400));

item.name: D · Invoice attach — negatives & policy
item.description: Run **folder C first** (these reuse `{{listing_id}}` and its attached artifact). Each proves one rule.

exec: pm.test('400 — must be a stored PDF (DOC.5)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(400));

item.name: D3 Attach to an unknown listing → 404

request.description: Run '1 · Login → Login: Compliance' first so bearer_compliance is set.

item.name: D6 Second active attach → 400 already active (DOC.1)

exec: pm.test('400 — one active artifact per invoice', () => pm.response.to.have.status(400));

item.name: E · Replace / supersede (DOC.7)
item.description: Run **folder C first**. Upload a replacement PDF, then supersede the active artifact — the old one goes `superseded`, the new one becomes `active` (never a hard delete).

item.name: E5 List → new active, old superseded
F · KYC document upload (BC11) — advanced
item.description: Same upload → attach pattern over a KYC file (M20). **Requires a `kyc_file_id`**, which is created by supplier/investor onboarding (`5 · Supplier onboarding` up to *Submit KYC*). There is no read endpoint for the id in dev, so set the `kyc_file_id` collection variable manually (from the DB or app logs) before running F1. Uploads reuse the generic `/documents` flow from folder A.

exec: // Shared helper for the Login folder: password → fetch dev OTP → stash challenge_id + otp_code.
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ email: email, password: pm.collectionVariables.get('password') }) }
if (err || res.code !== 200) { console.log('login password failed', err, res && res.code, res && res.text()); return; }
if (e2 || r2.code !== 200) { console.log('otp fetch failed', e2, r2 && r2.code); return; }
// Helper: initiate a doc but never upload/finalize it -> stays pending_upload.
body: { mode:'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ kind:'invoice', content_type:'application/pdf', declared_size:512 }) }
if (err || res.code >= 300) { console.log('initPendingDoc failed', err, res && res.code, res && res.text()); return; }
// Helper: full initiate -> upload -> finalize; stores the stored document_id into varName.
const auth = function (extra) { return Object.assign({ 'Authorization':'Bearer '+bearer }, extra || {}); };
body: { mode:'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ kind:'invoice', content_type:'application/pdf', declared_size:64 }) }
if (e1 || r1.code >= 300) { console.log('uploadStoredPdf#init failed', e1, r1 && r1.text()); return; }
header: auth({ 'Content-Type':'application/pdf' }), body: { mode:'raw', raw: '%PDF-1.4\nfresh ' + id }
if (e2 || r2.code >= 300) { console.log('uploadStoredPdf#content failed', e2, r2 && r2.text()); return; }
pm.sendRequest({ url: base + '/documents/' + id + '/finalize', method: 'POST', header: auth() },
if (e3 || r3.code >= 300) { console.log('uploadStoredPdf#finalize failed', e3, r3 && r3.text()); return; }
